{{Short description="Principle that gravitational mass and inertial mass are equivalent}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Equivalence principle
| image = Equivalence principle.svg
| caption = An observer cannot locally distinguish between acceleration and a uniform gravitational field.
| topics = [[General relativity]], [[Gravity]], [[Mass]], [[Acceleration]]
| proposed_by = [[Albert Einstein]], 1907
}}

The '''equivalence principle''' is the fundamental postulate of [[general relativity]] stating that the effects of [[gravity]] are locally indistinguishable from those of [[acceleration]]. It was described by [[Albert Einstein]] as the "happiest thought of my life" (1907) and forms the conceptual foundation of general relativity.

== Weak equivalence principle (WEP) ==
All objects fall at the same rate in a gravitational field, regardless of their [[mass]] or composition — the universality of free fall.

For any two bodies:
:<math>\frac{m_i}{m_g} = \text{constant} = 1</math>

where <math>m_i</math> is [[inertial mass]] and <math>m_g</math> is [[gravitational mass]].

== Einstein equivalence principle (EEP) ==
In a freely falling reference frame, the laws of physics reduce to those of [[special relativity]] (no gravity). Equivalently:

"An observer in a windowless room cannot distinguish between being stationary on Earth and being in a rocket accelerating at <math>g = 9.81\ \text{m/s}^2</math> in deep space."

== Strong equivalence principle (SEP) ==
All laws of physics — not just mechanics — are the same in all freely falling frames. This implies that [[gravitational self-energy]] falls at the same rate as other mass.

== Consequences ==
* '''Gravitational redshift''' — light loses energy climbing out of gravity wells
* '''Gravitational time dilation''' — clocks run slower in stronger gravity
* '''Bending of light''' — light follows curved paths in gravity (confirmed by [[Eddington experiment]], 1919)
* '''Universality of free fall''' — Apollo 15: hammer and feather dropped simultaneously on the Moon

== Experimental tests ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Experiment !! Precision
|-
| [[Eötvös experiment]] || 10⁻⁹
|-
| Lunar laser ranging || 10⁻¹³
|-
| [[MICROSCOPE]] satellite (2017) || 10⁻¹⁵
|-
| Proposed [[STEP (satellite)|STEP]] || 10⁻¹⁸
|}

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[General relativity]]
* [[Gravity]]
* [[Mass]]
* [[Spacetime]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:General relativity]]
[[Category:Gravity]]
